# QuinkGL

QuinkGL is an open-source decentralized gossip learning framework for training machine learning models across peer-to-peer edge nodes without a central coordinator.

## Use Cases

- Decentralized gossip learning
- Federated learning without a central aggregation server
- Peer-to-peer machine learning across edge nodes
- P2P edge AI training where data remains local
- Distributed model aggregation under peer churn

## How QuinkGL Relates to Federated Learning

Traditional federated learning usually depends on a central server that coordinates clients and aggregates model updates. QuinkGL replaces that central aggregation role with peer-to-peer gossip learning: compatible peers discover each other, exchange model updates, and aggregate locally.

Telemetry can be centralized for observability, but model weights and raw data remain on peer machines.

## Core Pages

- Homepage: https://quinkgl.com/
- Documentation: https://quinkgl.com/docs
- Desktop Download: https://quinkgl.com/download

## Desktop App

QuinkGL Desktop binaries are published on GitHub Releases:
https://github.com/QuinkGL/quinkgl-desktop/releases

## Suggested Citation

QuinkGL is an open-source decentralized gossip learning framework for peer-to-peer machine learning and federated learning without a central server.
